title: Podman Desktop is not in sync with Podman CLI - Broken API
body:
### Issue Description

I am using podman on a work computer through a remote desktop connection from another work computer ( in the same network ). My podman desktop is not showing the containers that are running in podman cli, even if they are on the same connection. 

Also, whenever starting or stopping the podman machine I get these errors:

Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
API forwarding for Docker API clients is not available due to the following startup failures.
        could not start api proxy since expected pipe is not available: podman-machine-default

Podman clients are still able to connect.
Machine "podman-machine-default" started successfully

Could not stop API forwarding service (win-sshproxy.exe): open 
blablabla\containers\podman\machine\wsl\podman-machine-default\win-sshproxy.tid: The system cannot find the file specified.
Machine "podman-machine-default" stopped successfully


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. try to start or stop podman machine
2. try to run any containers in cli and check podman desktop


### Describe the results you received

Podman desktop is not showing pods or containers that are running in the CLI, even if they both use the same connection.

### Describe the results you expected

Show the existing containers and pods in the desktop application.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Trying to do this on podman running on a work machine that I access via another work machine remote connection.

### Additional information

Another coworker has no issues connecting to the same machine via remote connection and using podman.

comments.body: can you please try with the latest podman release v5.6.0  ?
`Could not stop API forwarding service (win-sshproxy.exe): open` generally means something networking related is off.  Can you ensure you are using NAT mode?

BTW, I do not consider the Podman Desktop to be part of this bug report, or maybe better put, it should be a different issue on Podman Desktop.  The fact that your machine does not start, which is pure podman, is certainly a problem.  Please use the latest Podman version, and then verify your networking is compatible.

Fixed it by following these steps:

1. Stop the VM

`podman machine stop`

2. Kill any stale Windows proxy and delete its state files

```
Get-Process win-sshproxy -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Stop-Process -Force

$base = "$env:USERPROFILE\.local\share\containers\podman\machine\wsl\podman-machine-default"
Remove-Item -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue -Force `
  (Join-Path $base 'win-sshproxy.tid'),
  (Join-Path $base 'win-sshproxy.pid')
```

3. Start the VM

`podman machine start`

4. Check the pipe was created

`[System.IO.Directory]::GetFiles("\\.\pipe\") | ? { $_ -match "podman|docker|sshproxy" }`

Now this should work. Or at least it worked for me. A whole week of frustrations lmao, I for sure messed something up when not paying attention. Hope this helps someone else.

Cheers!
